title: Trend Entity
description: Schema for validating trend.md YAML frontmatter. Trends synthesize cross-finding patterns answering research questions. Structure depends on research_type: 'smarter-service' uses TIPS framework (Trend-Implication-Possibility-Solution); other types use STANDARD format (Context-Evidence-Implications).

tags.description: Classification tags including entity type, dimension, and planning horizon

dc:creator.description: Dublin Core creator field - skill that created the trend

dc:title.description: Dublin Core title field - trend name (2-4 words)

dc:identifier.description: Dublin Core identifier - unique trend ID with 6-8 char hash

dc:created.description: Dublin Core creation timestamp - ISO 8601 format

dc:description.description: Dublin Core description field - 1-2 sentence summary

research_type.description: Research type determining content structure (smarter-service uses TIPS, others use STANDARD)

synthesis_format.description: Content format type - TIPS for smarter-service, STANDARD for others

dimension.description: Primary dimension this trend relates to (slug format) - only for dimension-scoped mode

planning_horizon.description: Urgency classification based on evidence maturity - act (0-6 months, ready now), plan (6-18 months, capability building), observe (18+ months, emerging signals)

finding_refs.description: Array of wikilinks to findings supporting this trend

concept_refs.description: Array of wikilinks to concepts referenced in this trend

megatrend_refs.description: Array of wikilinks to megatrends this trend manifests

claim_refs.description: Array of wikilinks to claims supporting this trend (minimum 3 required)

addresses_questions.description: Array of refined question IDs this trend answers (traceability)

citation_count.description: Number of inline citations in the trend content

word_count.description: Total word count of trend content (target varies by format: 1050-1300 STANDARD, 800-1000 TIPS)

evidence_strength.description: How well-supported by claims and findings (0.0-1.0)

quality_rating.description: Overall quality classification based on composite score

evidence_freshness.description: Temporal validity assessment based on evidence age

oldest_evidence_date.description: ISO date of oldest cited claim (for freshness assessment)
